Recruiting activity
Viewed by Maheen Ashar
5/14/2026 · 3:21 PM

Summary

I build software that handles scale without breaking a sweat. Over the last 5+ years, I’ve worn a lot of hats: full-stack engineer, tech lead, and systems architect. But the core of what I do is always the same: I figure out how to make complex systems run faster, smarter, and deliver real business value. Lately, my focus is squarely on the intersection of AI and high-performance computing. I’m currently building with Rust and designing RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and LLM infrastructure. Everyone is talking about AI, but making it run efficiently, securely, and cheaply in production is the hard part, and that’s exactly what I focus on.A few things I’ve shipped that I'm proud of:1. Architected for speed: Built a 3D asset labeling workflow from the ground up that delivered a 200% efficiency improvement for a critical business operation. 2. Enterprise scale: Designed event-driven architectures that power cradle-to-grave analytics for massive global brands like Coca-Cola and Starbucks.3. High-throughput systems: Dived deep into the Web3 space early in my career, helping architect a DeFi exchange infrastructure that secured $35M in funding and handled $2M+ in daily trading volume.I’ve been a remote-first engineer for years, successfully leading technical initiatives and mentoring teams across the UK, Netherlands, Australia, and Pakistan. I know how to communicate across time-zones, bridge the gap between technical execution and business strategy, and make remote collaboration actually work.I’m always happy to connect with engineers, founders, and builders. If you’re working on something interesting at the intersection of AI infrastructure, systems programming, or complex distributed systems, let’s talk.Core Stack: Rust, AI/LLMs, RAG Architecture, MERN, Go, AWS, GCP, Docker, Kubernetes.#RustLang #RAG #LLMInfrastructure #AIEngineering #SystemsArchitecture #SeniorSoftwareEngineer #DistributedSystems #SoftwareArchitecture #MERNStack #RemoteEngineering

    Position summary
    • Infrastructure Ownership: Led end-to-end ownership of critical custom domain system, architecting DNS verification, SSL/Cloudflare integration, marketplace slug routing, and backend migration. 
    • Fixed race condition preventing data loss under concurrent table edits. 
    • Testing & Quality Leadership: Identified critical testing gap affecting 16+ untested critical paths (billing, auth, subscriptions, permissions). 
    • Authored RFC-001 (Testing & CI/CD Strategy)—comprehensive systems design addressing test pyramid, business risk prioritization, integration testing patterns (httpmock), and cost-effective CI infrastructure (WarpBuild, $40-80/month). 
    • Systematic Codebase Improvement: Built validation framework rolling across 20+ component types; established database testing patterns; designed deterministic test utilities; created testing guidelines balancing velocity with reliability. 
    • Technical Culture & Mentorship: Integrated 30+ code review improvements; documented architectural decisions (why, not just what); demonstrated restraint knowing when to align on foundational patterns vs. 
    • moving independently; mentored teammates on patterns and technical thinking. 
    • Systems Thinking Demonstrated: Mapped risk vs. 
    • coverage matrix identifying critical modules by business impact (billing > auth > user management); prioritized testing roadmap by severity; designed cost-effective infrastructure.

    Position summary
    In my role as a
    Senior Software Engineer
    at Abyss Solutions, I have undertaken impactful responsibilities: 
     
    1. Spearheaded the architecture and release of internal software, the 3D Asset Labelling workflow. This innovation is anticipated to yield a remarkable 200% efficiency improvement in a key internal process critical to the business. 
     
    2. Successfully designed and implemented an interactive 3D web-based application, showcasing proficiency in cutting-edge technologies. 
     
    3. Mentored and provided guidance to junior engineers, aiding in the evaluation of technical options, implementation of best practices, and effective troubleshooting. This initiative resulted in a notable 40% reduction in software bugs and a commendable 20% increase in overall team productivity. 
     
    4. Collaborated closely with diverse teams, spanning two locations in Pakistan and one in Australia, and product and business teams. This collaboration aimed at enhancing engineering and product processes within the team. 
     
    5. Effectively balanced competing business, user, and technical priorities, guiding decision-making on scope and releases. This strategic approach led to a substantial 25% reduction in development cycle time. 
     
    These accomplishments underscore my commitment to driving efficiency, fostering team growth, and contributing to the overall success of Abyss Solutions.

    • 3D Asset Labelling Architecture: Spearheaded the architecture and development of 3D Asset Labelling software, boosting process efficiency by 200%. 
    • Led technical decisions and team mentoring on architectural patterns. 
    • Reporting Module: Developed a reporting module that increased user engagement by 50%. 
    • Team Mentoring: Mentored engineering team on systems design and technical best practices.

    Position summary
    I played a pivotal role in various impactful initiatives: 
     
    1. Collaborated with the blockchain team to architect infrastructure, enhance security, and implement monitoring for Zenith Blockchain. This endeavor not only contributed to securing a substantial $35 million in funding but also facilitated a remarkable $2 million daily trading volume. 
     
    2. Led projects involving Stripe Integration, WebRTC, and Frontend development for diverse GPT-3 applications. Concurrently, I mentored and addressed challenges for interns and juniors, fostering a collaborative and innovative work environment. 
     
    3. Contributed significantly to the AI Content Generation Project by actively supporting its development cycle. 
     
    4. Successfully developed and deployed a Web3 Dapp application using Solidity, Moralis, Web3, and smart contracts, showcasing expertise in blockchain technologies. 
     
    5. Engineered and launched a dynamic Jupyterhub application for a client, leveraging Kubernetes to dynamically generate and delete pods for incoming users, enhancing scalability and efficiency. 
     
    6. Played a key role in streamlining the company's internal tools, specifically the Build system. This optimization not only saved valuable time for the company but also resulted in record deliveries by other departments. 
     
    These experiences highlight my comprehensive skill set and commitment to delivering impactful solutions, aligning with the dynamic demands of Full Stack development.
